---
title: "Day 9: AI-Assisted Modeling with RAG"
subtitle: "Morning brief, Thursday July 23"
author: "Dan Negrut"
date: 2026-07-23
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../theme.scss]
    slide-number: true
    width: 1250
    margin: 0.08
    transition: fade
    footer: "ProCSI C Training  |  Day 9"
    embed-resources: true
---

## Things happening today
- Come to the lab at 3:30 pm, will tell you a bit about the lab.
    - You will get your ProCSI t-shirt. We'll take a group photo.
    - One lab student will show how the AV team uses Chrono for testing autonomous vehicles.
- At 4:00 pm we have the usual "end-of-day" meeting, in the regular room.
- At 6:15 pm we meet on State Street at **State Street Brats** (603 State St).


## Where this fits

- Today is capstone day: Python, simulation, PyChrono, and the AI tutor.
- Claude is great, but it can misremember a specific library. 
- **RAG** (retrieval-augmented generation) fixes that: before answering, the AI **looks up the real documentation and source code**, then writes its answer from what it found.
- **chrono-rag** is RAG pointed at Project Chrono: it searches the actual Chrono code and demos, so the PyChrono scripts it produces use the real API and features of Chrono.

## Why do we talk about this?

- You can use this RAG idea on your own projects.
- Examples:
    - You can RAG all your course notes for a class you are taking.
    - You can RAG the documentation of a library you are using.

## Today's target

- **By 4:00, your team can:** use chrono-rag to generate a PyChrono model you did not write by hand (a script for a virtual experiment), **run it**, and explain to the rest of us what the model does.
- Challenge: run the VIPER but operating on SCM terrain (ask Claude with RAG about SCM)

## How to start

- Tools: your `chrono` conda environment with PyChrono from yesterday
- Follow the steps in the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/projectchrono/chrono-rag)
- **No starter file today; the AI is what gets you started.** 
    - Describe what you want simulated, in plain English, and iterate.
- Good first asks: a pendulum, a stack of boxes falling over, a ball bouncing down steps, your day-5 slider-crank.
- If a run crashes: paste the error back and ask for a fix. That loop, ask, run, read the error, ask again, is real engineering.

## In the afternoon: go bigger {.smaller}

- Scale up your morning model, or generate a fresh, more ambitious one with chrono-rag.
- Alternative: pick up an existing model from the Chrono demo collection on GitHub (projectchrono/chrono, under `src/demos/python/`) and bend it to your will.
- Judge the AI's output like an engineer: does the motion look physically right? Is anything too good to be true?
- Tomorrow morning:
    - The CSULA folks leave Madison. See you next year, Sal!!!
    - The rest of the crew: head to State Street. For lunch, try **Dotty Dumpling's Dowry**

## Your end-of-day talk

- ~10 minutes: run your generated model live, tell us what you asked for, what you got, and what you had to fix.
- **Start or Close with your team's quote of the day.**
- Stretch goal: have chrono-rag generate the VIPER rover running on SCM terrain.
